issue_body:
Let creators define a reusable brand kit for each show, including logo, colors, type style, caption style, and common overlay assets, then make that kit available when starting or styling an episode. This turns the existing show library and template saving into a stronger repeatable identity system for teams managing multiple podcasts.

## Acceptance (definition of done)
A reviewer can create or edit a show brand kit from the show library, see the chosen logo/colors/type/caption style reflected in preset preview and the episode workspace, and confirm an exported episode summary uses the selected brand kit. Existing episode setup, template, style, workspace, and export flows still pass `node scripts/run-tests.mjs`, with focused coverage for saving, loading, and applying a brand kit.

---
This is the current active step for this product. The maintainer will only merge a pull request that completes THIS step and demonstrably works. Open a PR that closes this issue.

_Why this step now: The product already has setup, presets, canvas editing, audio polish, visual moments, templates, export, review, workspace guidance, and a show library. The next most valuable gap is making each show feel distinct and repeatable without rebuilding visual choices every episode, which is central to the Canva-for-podcasts vision._
